title: IMGUI Input Routing
use_for: routing IMGUI events between windows, panels, and widgets
defining focus ownership and event consumption rules
designing command dispatch for tool UI
bridging Unity Input System actions and device state into IMGUI framework commands
do_not_use_for: architectures that depend on legacy Input Manager reads as the primary signal path
rules: treat focus and hot control as framework-owned concerns
separate command dispatch from raw event inspection
derive framework commands from Input System before dispatching to IMGUI surfaces
support projects where legacy Input Manager is disabled
procedure: Identify host-level event entry and Input System action sources.
Translate Input System actions or device reads into framework command intent.
Route events through explicit window and panel boundaries.
Consume or forward events according to deterministic rules.

primary_when: the request is specifically about event routing, focus, or command dispatch in IMGUI
must_not_own_when: the request is mainly about layout composition or visual structure

solves: who handles which IMGUI events and when
how focus and active control move between UI regions
how modern Input System signals become host, panel, and widget commands

core_principles: Route high-level commands through framework boundaries, not ad hoc widget logic.
Keep focus state inspectable and host-visible.
Do not let arbitrary feature widgets silently consume shared events.
Prefer Input System action maps or explicit device reads as the source of command intent in modern Unity.
Treat IMGUI Event inspection as a downstream UI concern, not the only source of user intent.
constraints: Input handling must respect IMGUI event phases.
Competing widgets require deterministic precedence rules.
Mouse and keyboard ownership must be debuggable.
The routing layer must still function when old Input Manager axes and buttons are unavailable.
decision_rules: Handle host-level shortcuts before nested panels when the command is global.
Keep panel-local shortcuts inside panel routing when they should not leak to the host.
Instrument routing before adding more abstraction if focus bugs appear.
Prefer InputAction and InputActionMap for reusable host commands and tool-local commands.
Use raw device reads from Input System only when action maps are too indirect for the interaction.

preferred_patterns: explicit event routers at host and panel boundaries
focus state stored in retained host state
command tables separate from raw Event inspection
host-level input adapter that converts Input System signals into framework commands
action-map ownership aligned with window or tool lifetime
avoid_patterns: event use spread through unrelated draw helpers
silent global shortcut handling inside leaf widgets
focus inferred only from transient draw context
command routing built primarily on UnityEngine.Input polling
mixing legacy Input Manager assumptions into framework contracts

recipes.name: Route global and panel-local shortcuts through Input System
recipes.when: Use when a tool window has both host commands and content-specific commands and the project relies on Input System.
steps: Define host-level and panel-level action ownership explicitly.
Resolve Input System actions into framework commands before nested IMGUI drawing.
Forward unhandled events into the active panel.
Allow the panel to consume only its declared commands.

pitfalls: Leaf widgets consuming events that should remain global.
Shortcut handling duplicated across host and panel levels.
No retained representation of focus ownership.
Assuming IMGUI Event alone is a sufficient abstraction over modern Unity input.
Hard-coding legacy input axis or button names into framework routing.
review_checklist: Are focus transitions visible in the design?
Can command precedence be explained without reading every widget?
Is event consumption limited to clear boundaries?
Can the routing layer run with Input System as the only active backend?
Are command sources separated from raw IMGUI Event inspection?

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI Event Cycle
references.interpretation: Use this first for host-level event routing, focus ownership, and the relationship between IMGUI events and framework command flow.
references.why: Use for host-level event routing, focus ownership, and control-flow guidance.

references.title: Local Reference: IMGUI Input System Integration
references.interpretation: Use this first when building action-based routing or supporting projects with legacy input disabled.
references.why: Use for action-based command routing and modern Unity projects where legacy input is disabled.

references.title: Unity Scripting API: Event
references.interpretation: Use this to confirm low-level event semantics after the domain's routing model is already established.
references.why: Use for event types, consumption, and input details that drive routing decisions.

references.title: Input System Manual: Actions
references.interpretation: Use this to confirm action maps, callbacks, and polling patterns that the framework adapts into command routing.
references.why: Use for action maps, action ownership, callbacks, and polling patterns when building framework-level command routing.

references.title: Input System Manual: Installation
references.interpretation: Use this to confirm Active Input Handling requirements when the framework must run without the legacy Input Manager.
references.why: Use for the active input handling requirement when projects disable the legacy Input Manager and rely on Input System.
